response:
Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters from `/shared/config/helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml` and put together a quick summary.

[Calling write_report(title="Compassionate-Use Dosing Summary for Dr. Martinez", content="# Project Helios — Compassionate-Use Dosing Summary

**Source:** `/shared/config/helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`  
**Last updated:** 2026-03-28  
**Compound:** NXB-7042  
**Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)

## Patient dosing summary

| Patient ID | Age (months) | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated Dose (mg) | Frequency | Next Dose | PK Clearance (mL/min) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 | 12.4 | 0.15 | 1.86 | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 | Stable. Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |
| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

## Quick PK status

Based on the latest dosing config, there are **no obvious immediate PK red flags** in the current compassionate-use parameters:

- All three patients have active patient-specific clearance values recorded.
- Clinical notes for **CU-001** and **CU-002** indicate stable or improving response.
- **CU-003** is a new enrollment, so parameters appear current, but closer follow-up is appropriate given the early treatment stage.

**Caveat:** This summary reflects the latest stored dosing configuration and patient PK clearance entries; it is **not a full rerun or formal validation of the PK models**.
